name: Monterey Bay Aquarium
highlights: One of the world's great marine science institutions — opened 1984 on the site of a historic sardine cannery
Living Kelp Forest exhibit: a three-story tank of the actual ecosystem you swim above in Monterey Bay
Sea Otter Program rehabilitates and releases wild otters — often visible in the Great Tide Pool
Seafood Watch app helps travelers make sustainable seafood choices at restaurants everywhere

name: Henry Miller Memorial Library
highlights: A clearing in the redwoods — bookshop, gallery, and community gathering place
Regular readings, concerts, and film screenings in the outdoor amphitheater
Curated selection of Miller's work and books about the Big Sur experience
The cultural soul of Big Sur, maintained by volunteers since 1981

name: Robinson Jeffers' Tor House
highlights: Stone cottage and Hawk Tower built entirely by hand by poet Robinson Jeffers, 1914–1963
Jeffers carried each stone up from Carmel Beach and set them himself — the house is a poem
Saturday tours only; small groups; advance reservation required
Jeffers's poetry defined the Big Sur coast as sacred terrain — this is the source
admission: paid — reserve through Tor House Foundation

name: Point Lobos State Natural Reserve
highlights: Called 'the greatest meeting of land and water in the world' by landscape photographer Edward Weston
One of only two remaining groves of native Monterey cypress on Earth
Sea lions, harbor seals, sea otters, and migrating gray whales visible from shore
Reservation required for parking — book well in advance in summer

name: Carmel Mission Basilica
highlights: Founded in 1771 by Father Junípero Serra — the most historically significant site in the orbit
Serra is buried here; the mission was the headquarters of the California mission system
Museum with original artifacts; the basilica itself is architecturally stunning
Rose garden and courtyard offer quiet even during busy periods
